classification.loc_class: QC - Physics: plasma and electromagnetic fields

theory.canonical_statement: ID3850 records Dimensionless Gyromagnetic Constant Relation as a source-extracted canonical equation.
theory.technical_interpretation: The entry preserves the equation exactly as extracted from `D:\00_Engine\CSV\registry_repaired.csv` for registry alignment and later derivation.

latex_blocks: $$\mu$, where $\mu$ is the magnetic moment and M,R,$\Omega$M,R,\OmegaM,R,$\Omega$ as in ID\,044. In the Trawinistic framework, $\g_\mu$ is predicted to be O(1)\mathcal\{O\}(1)O(1) across systems.\\\",,"Lemma (Dimensionless Gyromagnetic Coupling Constant). The dimensionless gyromagnetic coupling constant $\g_\mu$ is defined by g\_$\mu$=$\mu$M R2 $\Omega$g\textbackslash\_\{\mu\}=\frac\{\mu\}\{M\textbackslash,R\^{}2\textbackslash,\Omega\}g\_$\mu$=MR2$\Omega$$
canonical_equation.source_section_latex: $$\mu$, where $\mu$ is the magnetic moment and M,R,$\Omega$M,R,\OmegaM,R,$\Omega$ as in ID\,044. In the Trawinistic framework, $\g_\mu$ is predicted to be O(1)\mathcal\{O\}(1)O(1) across systems.\\\",,"Lemma (Dimensionless Gyromagnetic Coupling Constant). The dimensionless gyromagnetic coupling constant $\g_\mu$ is defined by g\_$\mu$=$\mu$M R2 $\Omega$g\textbackslash\_\{\mu\}=\frac\{\mu\}\{M\textbackslash,R\^{}2\textbackslash,\Omega\}g\_$\mu$=MR2$\Omega$$
